title: LUC-30 Surjectivity Pattern — BSD Shadow Bridge on Hodge

analysis.object: T727: LUC-30 Surjectivity Pattern — BSD Shadow Bridge on Hodge depth analysis

bsd_surjectivity_analog.description: BSD surjectivity: every L-zero produces rational point

hodge_surjectivity_analog.description: Hodge surjectivity: every Hodge class produces algebraic cycle

shadow_bridge_bsd.description: BSD shadow bridge: Heegner point = EML-2 shadow of L-zero

shadow_bridge_hodge.description: Hodge shadow bridge: algebraic cycle = EML-0 shadow of Hodge class?
shadow_bridge_hodge.reason: Shadow from EML-3 to EML-0 requires crossing TYPE3 gap

depth_differential.description: BSD gap: EML-2 to EML-0 = finite jump; Hodge gap: EML-3 to EML-0 = larger
depth_differential.reason: Hodge gap is strictly deeper than BSD proved gap

luc_universality.description: LUC says all natural dualities follow same pattern
luc_universality.reason: LUC-30 instance should behave like LUC-15 (BSD)

pattern_break.description: LUC-30 may be the FIRST LUC instance that fails -- depth too large
pattern_break.reason: Or confirms LUC universality extends to TYPE3 gaps

analysis.theorem: T727: LUC-30 Surjectivity Pattern — BSD Shadow Bridge on Hodge (S1006).

key_theorem: T727: LUC-30 Surjectivity Pattern — BSD Shadow Bridge on Hodge (S1006).
